bullet_points: Fetch MX2 compounds (M = Mo, W, Nb, Ta, Ti, Zr; X = S, Se, Te) via Materials Project API.
Parse lattice_parameters into individual numeric columns (a, b, c, alpha, beta, gamma).
Perform integrity audit: check for NaNs, duplicates (unique material_id), infinite values, and verify energy_above_hull >= 0 and band_gap >= 0.
Save the cleaned dataset to tmd_data.csv and print the file path, shape, and sample rows to the console.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Data acquisition, parsing, and integrity audit

bullet_points: Compute descriptive statistics (mean, std, min, max, quartiles) for all numeric columns.
Calculate and print the full correlation matrix including lattice parameters.
Report counts of thermodynamically stable materials (energy_above_hull == 0) and metallic phases (band_gap == 0).
Compute skewness and kurtosis for dos_at_fermi and energy_above_hull, and provide recommendations on log-scaling for downstream modeling.

Generate a four-panel figure: Distribution of dos_at_fermi across top space groups, Scatter plot of energy_above_hull vs. band_gap, Box plots of dos_at_fermi by transition metal, Heatmap of mean dos_at_fermi (transition metal vs. top space groups).
Save as tmd_analysis_plots.png and print the list of included space groups, metals, and their respective counts to the console.

Formulate clarifying questions regarding: dos_at_fermi units (states/eV/unit-cell vs. states/eV/atom), API query scope (polymorphs vs. ground-state), and spacegroup definition (conventional vs. primitive).
Synthesize all findings into a final report.
Detail data quality diagnostics (missing rates, outliers, skewness).
Integrate heatmap insights to identify high-potential metal-symmetry combinations for superconductivity.
